homepage.title: Uma Kumaran
headings: h2: Working hard for Stratford and Bow
homepage.text_excerpt: Uma Kumaran 0 Skip to Content About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Open Menu Close Menu About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Open Menu Close Menu About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Working hard for Stratford and Bow Welcome to my website – I’m Uma Kumaran and I’m the Member of Parliament for Stratford and Bow. About me I was born in East London, I’ve lived in Stratford and in Bow, I’ve studied here and now I’m so proud to represent the community here. I ran to be your MP because I want to be your voice, on your side at the heart of government; and to give back to the place that means so much to me. 14 years of Tory austerity had a huge impact on our communities and our country. But I know the difference Labour in power can make. From creating our NHS to providing free school meals to workers’ rights – it’s a Labour Government that delivered life changing policies for millions of people in Britain. As your voice in Westminster, working with this Labour Government, I’ll always stand up for you and deliver for you and your family. My story: The story of East London is a tapestry of cultures and communities. My own parents lived in Tower Hamlets and then Newham when they first came to Britain over 40 years ago. Their story is one of hope and perseverance – it’s one I have grown up knowing all my life – guiding my values in all I do. They came to Britain fleeing pogroms in Sri Lanka in search of safety, with the hope and aspiration for a better tomorrow. Britain offered them and many Tamils special refuge and safety, to rebuild their lives and start again free from the fear of persecution. Their story is part of Britain’s story. It’s part of my story. And it’s a story that can be found in every corner of Stratford and Bow and in every part of Britain. From Kindertransport, to the windrush generation, to Bengali migrants, to homes for Ukraine – we have a proud history of helping those in need. I am determined to give back and to create the life chances that make it possible for all children in Stratford and Bow to succeed – no matter what their start in life and to make Stratford and Bow the best place to live, work and invest in. My work in parliament: Before entering Parliament, I worked internationally to advance bolder climate action, as the Director of Diplomatic and International Relations at a global climate organisation. I worked with mayors and cities around the world, the United Nations, civil society and trade unions to push for stronger, more ambitious action to counter the threat of climate change. In Parliament, I carry this work forward as the Vice Chair of the All Party Group [APPG] on International conservation, the Vice Chair of PRASEG the APPG on renewable and sustainable energy, the Co-chair of the APPG on Waterways and a member of the APPGs on Climate and Environment. I am an elected member of Parliament’s Foreign Affairs Committee using my international experience to hold our government’s foreign policy to account, to ask the tough questions and to do my part to make sure that Britain’s role on a global stage is one we can be proud of. I have been appointed by the Prime Minister as a member of the UK-EU Parliamentary Assembly , taking my voice to Brussels and pushing forward on closer ties and friendship between the UK and Europe, advocating for youth mobility and better trading relationship. I am also on the board of Best for Britain’s Trade and Business commission, a group working to strengthen trade of our nations and regions with the EU. I am a Labour MP because my values are Labour values. I was born in the NHS and have worked for the NHS. I've spent most of my working life in public service, from working with the UN and global climate advocates to tackle climate change, to helping London's Mayor deliver life-changi

pages.title: Contact — Uma Kumaran
headings: h2: Need to discuss your situation? Want to share your views and help shape my work?
pages.text_excerpt: Contact — Uma Kumaran 0 Skip to Content About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Open Menu Close Menu About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Open Menu Close Menu About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Contact me Need to discuss your situation? Want to share your views and help shape my work? The quickest way to contact me is by email at uma.kumaran.mp@parliament.uk Speak to my office by calling 020 7219 7777 Write to me at: House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA. Please include your: Full name Full Address and Postcode (Stratford and Bow) Information about the problem you’re facing. My team and I can only help if you provide all this information as, with in line with parliamentary protocol, I can only make representations on behalf of those living in Stratford and Bow. Find out if I am your MP here . How I can help Note: If your issue relates to an ongoing enquiry or complaint with an independent body, organisation or agency please note that I cannot intervene or help until all their complaints and appeals processes have been exhausted. What I Can Do: ✅ I can raise matters which impact the constituency on your behalf in the House of Commons. ✅ I can write to relevant departments or officials on your behalf to aid in enquiries or complaints if necessary. ✅ I can write to secretaries of state and other ministers regarding relevant issues once all other channels of enquiry or complaint have been exhausted. What I Can’t Do: ❌ I cannot help in settling disputes between family members, neighbours, or between yourself and an employer. ❌ I cannot give legal advice or interfere in court cases or any decisions previously made in a court of law. ❌ I cannot provide any financial or business advice. Privacy policy Privacy policy Promoted by Aline Delawa on behalf of Uma Kumaran, both at 57-59 Great Suffolk Street, SE1 0BB Office standards Office standards

pages.title: Housing Help — Uma Kumaran
pages.text_excerpt: Housing Help — Uma Kumaran 0 Skip to Content About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Open Menu Close Menu About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Open Menu Close Menu About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Need Help With Housing? Note: Newham Council and Tower Hamlets Council have responsibility for the majority of housing issues including allocations, repairs and problems with landlords. If you are not satisfied with their response you may contact me, however please try to include the Council's response in your email. How I can help: ✅ I can make representations to housing associations or local councils where repairs have been delayed or refused, despite obligations. ✅ I can refer your case to the Housing Ombudsman if you have completed the final stage of your housing provider’s complaints process. ✅ I can raise concerns on your behalf where poor property management is affecting your health, safety, or wellbeing. What I can’t do: ❌ I cannot change an outcome where the council has offered accommodation. ❌ I cannot arrange housing for you if you are homeless. ❌ I cannot intervene with or change your position on a housing waiting list. FAQs I am unsatisfied with my position on a housing waiting list, can you help? I cannot intervene with or change your position on a housing waiting list. I would encourage anyone in this position to continue to bid on available properties. I am able to contact housing providers and the council on your behalf but I cannot change given outcomes. I’m having issues with repairs and maintenance, can you help? If you are living in a council property please contact your local councillor first. If you are in a housing association property please contact your housing provider first. If you are unsatisfied with the response from your landlord or housing provider please get in contact with my office where we can raise the issue on your behalf. Need to Discuss Your Situation with Me? The quickest way to contact me is by email at uma.kumaran.mp@parliament.uk Speak to my office by calling 020 7219 7777 Write to Uma at: House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA. Please include your: Full name Full Address and Postcode (Stratford and Bow) Information about the problem you’re facing. My team and I can only help if you provide all this information as, in line with parliamentary protocol, she can only help those living in Stratford and Bow. Find out if she is your MP here . Privacy policy Privacy policy Promoted by Aline Delawa on behalf of Uma Kumaran, both at 57-59 Great Suffolk Street, SE1 0BB Office standards Office standards

pages.title: Local Authority Issues — Uma Kumaran
pages.h1: Need Help With Local Authority Issues?
headings: h2: You can contact your council here:
h2: Need to Discuss Your Situation With Me?
pages.text_excerpt: Local Authority Issues — Uma Kumaran 0 Skip to Content About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Open Menu Close Menu About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Open Menu Close Menu About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Need Help With Local Authority Issues? Note: If you’ve contacted your local council and haven’t received a response — or feel your complaint hasn’t been handled properly — I may be able to make enquiries on your behalf or help escalate the issue. You should always try contacting the council directly first, as this is often the fastest way to resolve the matter. If you’re having trouble with council services - from parking fines to missed bin collections - I may be able to help. Many of the day-to-day services we all rely on, such as waste collection, street maintenance, Council Tax, and parking enforcement, are the responsibility of your local council - not Parliament. As the Member of Parliament for Stratford and Bow, I represent an area served by two local authorities: Newham Council Tower Hamlets Council If you’re not sure which borough you live in, you can check using your postcode here . You can contact your council here: Tower Hamlets Website: Contact us Phone: 020 7364 5000 Write to: Tower Hamlets Town Hall, 160 Whitechapel Road. London, E1 1BJ Newham Website: Contact and information – Newham Council Phone: 020 8430 2000 Write to: London Borough of Newham, Newham Dockside, 1000 Dockside Road, London, E16 2QU Need to Discuss Your Situation With Me? What I Can Do: ✅ If you’ve contacted your local council and haven’t received a response - or feel your complaint hasn’t been handled properly - I may be able to make enquiries on your behalf or help escalate the issue. The quickest way to contact me is by email at uma.kumaran.mp@parliament.uk Speak to my office by calling 020 7219 7777 Write to Uma at: House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA. Please include your: Full name Full Address and Postcode (Stratford and Bow) Information about the problem you’re facing. My team and I can only help if you provide all this information as, in line with parliamentary protocol, she can only help those living in Stratford and Bow. Find out if she is your MP here . What I Can’t Do: ❌ MPs are not permitted to intervene in ongoing legal matters, including court proceedings related to: Parking appeals Fixed penalty notices Noise abatement orders Council Tax court enforcement Privacy policy Privacy policy Promoted by Aline Delawa on behalf of Uma Kumaran, both at 57-59 Great Suffolk Street, SE1 0BB Office standards Office standards

pages.title: FAQs 1 — Uma Kumaran
headings: h3: If you live in Stratford & Bow, as your MP, Uma may be able to help with a problem you are having.
pages.text_excerpt: FAQs 1 — Uma Kumaran 0 Skip to Content About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Open Menu Close Menu About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact Open Menu Close Menu About me Newsletter Working hard for Stratford & Bow Speaking up in Parliament Policy Survey Contact If you live in Stratford & Bow, as your MP, Uma may be able to help with a problem you are having. The quickest way to contact Uma is by email at uma.kumaran.mp@parliament.uk Speak to her office by calling 020 7219 7777 Write to Uma at : House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA. Make sure you include your name, your full address in the constituency and some information about the problem you are having. Uma cannot help you unless you provide this information as, in line with parliamentary protocol, she can only help those living in Stratford and Bow. Find out if she is your MP here . Advice Surgeries The quickest way to deal with many problems is by email but should your case benefit from speaking to Uma directly, she holds regular advice surgeries for new cases by telephone, online and in person by appointment. When appointments are held in person, security personnel will be present at the venue in line with the latest guidance to keep Members of Parliament safe whilst they are carrying out their duties as an elected representative. To arrange an appointment , please email Uma and include details of your problem as well as your full address in the constituency. If you do not have access to email, please call Uma’s office on 020 7219 7777. Please read the information below before requesting an appointment. What issues can my MP help with? As a Member of Parliament, Uma is best able to help on issues that Parliament and central Government are responsible for. That includes things like problems with benefits or pensions, delays in the Home Office or concerns about the National Health Service. Uma can’t fix everything, but she can promise that she will do everything she can to assist or link you up with the right organisation to help. However, Uma is unable to help personally with private disputes between neighbours or employers, ongoing legal cases or overturning decisions made by the courts. Can Uma help if my concern is with Newham or Tower Hamlets Council? MPs don’t have any remit over councils or role in their decision making. For any council issues, including social housing, rubbish collections, education, or planning, in the first instance you should contact your locally elected Councillors who hold regular advice surgeries if you wish to chat to them in person. You can find out who they are here . In both Newham and Tower Hamlets , you also have directly elected Mayors who have overall responsibility for the council and the services they provide. If you have already taken these steps and remain unhappy, please provide Uma with copies of your correspondence and any responses received. Can Uma help with my immigration case? MPs are legally unable to provide immigration advice so it’s vital you seek specialist advice . However, Uma can make enquiries about the progress of applications with UK Visas and Immigration. She can only do so in instances where a decision has not been made within the standard timescale stated by the Home Office for their turnaround of applications. Can Uma help if she is not my MP? No, even if the problem you experienced happened in Stratford & Bow. Very strict rules known as ‘parliamentary protocol’ dictate that MPs can only help people who live in the constituency they represent. If you are a landlord with several properties, your MP is the one for your home address. The FindYourMP tool will provide details for your own MP and you should contact them. How quickly will I receive a response? Uma receives hundreds of emails every single day. Along with her small team, she works to ensure that all enquiries are dealt with as qu
